items.q: How does ADH affect the collecting duct in the kidney?
items.correct: It increases the permeability of the collecting duct to water (by inserting aquaporins), so more water is reabsorbed and urine is more concentrated.
items.misconception: ADH actively pumps water out of urine into the blood.
items.random_wrong: ADH directly opens holes in the collecting duct that let urine escape.
items.why: Students think hormones must 'do' something dramatic; they miss that ADH just changes membrane permeability, allowing osmosis to do the work.

items.q: Which direction does water move in the xylem, and what drives it?
items.correct: Upwards from roots to leaves — driven mainly by transpiration pull (evaporation at leaves creating tension on the water column).
items.misconception: Water moves up the xylem because roots pump it actively using ATP.
items.random_wrong: Water moves both directions in the xylem depending on the time of day.
items.why: Students assume an active 'pump' is needed; they don't realise evaporation at the top, combined with water's cohesion, pulls the whole column up.

items.correct: An undifferentiated cell capable of dividing to produce more stem cells or differentiating into specialised cell types.
items.misconception: Stem cells are taken only from embryos and cannot be found in adults.
items.random_wrong: Stem cells are simply cells that haven't grown big enough yet.
items.why: Media coverage of embryonic stem cells overshadows adult stem cells, so students think 'stem cell' = 'embryo cell'.

items.q: Which mutation type shifts the reading frame?
items.why: It's Frameshift insertion. “Silent” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In gel electrophoresis, DNA moves toward which electrode?
items.why: Correct answer: Anode (+). “Cathode (-)” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: PCR starts with 1 DNA molecule. After 20 cycles, how many copies are produced (approximately)?
items.why: The real figure is 1048576. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
